headline: Is BP (LSE:BP.) Still Too Expensive After Its 133% Run?
summary: BP’s share price has delivered a 133.4% return over the past 5 years, yet on Simply Wall St’s checks the stock screens as overvalued on earnings multiples and carries a low overall value score. This may give some investors pause after such a strong run. A 133.4% return over 5 years means BP has already created substantial shareholder value over this period, so fresh buyers need to think carefully about how much of the story might already be reflected in the price. Recent portfolio moves,...

headline: ConocoPhillips (COP) Buys 42% Of BP's Kirkuk Oil Fields In Iraq
summary: ConocoPhillips (NYSE:COP) has agreed to acquire a 42% stake in BP's Kirkuk oil fields in Iraq. The deal gives the company access to substantial oil reserves in one of the Middle East's historically important producing regions. The transaction adds another large international asset to ConocoPhillips' portfolio and may influence its long term production profile. ConocoPhillips enters this deal with its stock at $114.71 and a record of multi year performance, including a 145.9% return over the...

headline: Oil and Gas Employment Hits a 2026 Low Even as Production Sets Records
summary: U.S. oil and gas jobs hit a 2026 low even as production sets records, and the reasons have little to do with falling oil prices.

headline: ExxonMobil Stock Offers a Different Kind of Fuel
summary: This energy giant has been a standout performer, but its real value to your portfolio lies in how it moves, or doesn't move, with the market.
